title: Probabilistic weather forecasting with machine learning (GenCast)
authors: Ilan Price, Alvaro Sanchez-Gonzalez et al. (DeepMind)
description: Nature 637:84-90, Jan 2025. Outperforms ECMWF ENS on 97.2% of 1,320 targets; 15-day global ensemble in 8 minutes on 1 TPU v5.

title: AIFS -- ECMWF's data-driven forecasting system
description: First fully operational ML weather model from a national met center; 1000x more energy-efficient than IFS; TC track improvements ~20%. Operational Feb/Jul 2025.
note: Update: arXiv:2509.18994. Underpins open-source Anemoi framework.

title: Aurora: A Foundation Model for the Earth System
description: Nature 641:1180-1187, May 2025. 1.3B-param foundation model trained on >1M hours; outperforms operational forecasts on air quality, waves, TCs at 5000x speedup; demonstrates atmospheric scaling laws.

title: Neural General Circulation Models for Weather and Climate (NeuralGCM)
description: Nature 632:1060-1066, Aug 2024. Hybrid physics-ML GCM competitive for 1-15 day forecasts; multi-decade climate simulations with realistic TC frequency. Precipitation update: Science Advances 12(2), Jan 2026.

title: NOAA AIGFS / Project EAGLE
authors: Tabas et al.; Wang et al.
description: First operational hybrid physical-AI ensemble at national weather service; AIGFS uses 0.3% of GFS compute. Operational Dec 2025.

title: Can AI weather models predict out-of-distribution gray swan tropical cyclones?
authors: Y. Qiang Sun, Pedram Hassanzadeh et al.
description: PNAS 122(21), May 2025. FourCastNet trained without Cat 3-5 TCs cannot extrapolate to forecast Cat 5 storms -- relaxes toward training distribution. Cross-regional transfer works.
note: Fundamental concern for climate change scenarios.

title: Numerical models outperform AI weather forecasts of record-breaking extremes
authors: Ziming Zhang, Erich Fischer et al.
description: AI models (GraphCast, Pangu, FuXi) consistently show larger forecast errors for record-breaking events vs ECMWF HRES. Cited in Nature 651:583, 2026.

title: On Some Limitations of Current ML Weather Prediction Models
description: GRL 51(12), 2024. ML models lack physical consistency; excessive damping at high wavenumbers; skill advantage attributable to smoothing peculiarities.

title: Can AI-Based Weather Prediction Models Simulate the Butterfly Effect?
description: Pangu-Weather cannot reproduce butterfly effect; error growth rate structurally different from physics models -- incorrectly suggests unlimited predictability.

title: Impact of internal variability on benchmarking deep learning climate emulators
description: JAMES 2025. Linear pattern scaling beats ClimaX (100M params) on 3/4 climate variables; DL overfits to internal variability noise.

title: Can AI models reliably forecast extreme weather events?
description: Argues more rigorous testing required before widespread operational AI adoption; cites gray swan and record-breaking failures.

title: AI for modeling and understanding extreme weather and climate events
description: Comprehensive review: limited data, biases, need for transparent/reliable AI for extremes. Multi-EU-project collaboration (XAIDA, CLINT, AI4PEX).

title: Hierarchical Testing of a Hybrid ML-Physics Global Atmosphere Model
description: NeuralGCM captures global-scale warming responses under moderate forcing but diverges under +4K SST increase; dynamical core constrains but doesn't fully enable generalization.
note: First systematic evaluation of hybrid model climate-change generalization.

title: Physics-Based vs AI Weather Prediction: Atmospheric River Assessment
description: ML models show lower RMSE for AR variables but IFS HRES outperforms for identifying individual AR events within 4-day leads; lower RMSE != better event accuracy.

title: Crowdsourcing the Frontier: Advancing Hybrid Physics-ML Climate Simulation (ClimSim Kaggle)
authors: Jerry Lin, Zeyuan Hu et al.
description: Online stability now reproducibly achievable; but offline skill doesn't always transfer to online performance -- architectures respond differently.

title: WeatherBench 2: A benchmark for the next generation of data-driven global weather models
description: The primary public benchmark for all SOTA weather model comparisons in 2025-26.

title: ClimSim: An open large-scale dataset for training high-resolution climate emulators
description: Billion-scale samples, still the most-used subgrid benchmark in 2025-26.

title: Stable Machine-Learning Parameterization of Subgrid Processes
description: ClimSim online stability key baseline paper.

title: Rewiring climate modeling with machine learning emulators
description: Review/roadmap, suitable for writing a 2026 research landscape.
